reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Code reading is factually correct: lines 164-165 do exit mid-block when quota reaches zero. However, this is not exploitable. The spec (SG-008 INV-004) states 'All sidecars of a block must be included if any are included,' but this is a protocol compliance issue, not a security vulnerability. Impact analysis: (1) Partial columns are not wasted—they remain usable and the peer can request missing columns from other peers or retry the same peer later. (2) PeerDAS reconstruction requires 65/128 columns; receiving partial columns (e.g., 60) does not prevent eventual reconstruction after fetching 5 more from elsewhere. (3) This is intentional rate limiting behavior (MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS quota enforcement) that prevents a single request from monopolizing bandwidth. (4) No attacker-exploitable path exists—this affects normal protocol operation equally. Severity calibration: Prysm has ~31% consensus client share. Even if this affected all Prysm nodes, impact is <33% threshold for High severity. Given no exploitable attack path and peer can recover by re-requesting, this is a spec deviation with negligible security impact. Classification: Informational (protocol inefficiency, not vulnerability).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Fulu P2P Interface SG-008 INV-004: 'All sidecars of a block must be included if any are included.' Code violates this invariant by exiting mid-block at quota exhaustion (L164-165), but violation has no exploitable security impact.
